services.name: Incident Management
services.description: Unplanned interruptions and defects tracked as incidents (conventionally INC-prefixed identifiers in this model). This service_id is the default target when the user is viewing or discussing an active incident in the bridge or UI context, when ticket_id encodes an incident, or when the planner resolves narrative intent to reactive break-fix work. Agents and tools parameterized by service_id must use incident here whenever the underlying record is an incident—not a request, problem, or change.

services.name: Service Request Management
services.description: Formal service requests and catalog-driven asks (conventionally REQ-prefixed identifiers). Distinct from incidents: requests represent approved, repeatable, or fulfillment-style work rather than unplanned outages. Use this service_id when ticket_id or context points at a request record, or when the user’s goal is standard provisioning, access, or catalog fulfillment expressed as a service request. Summary, triage, and action agents that accept service_id must align with this module when operating on request entities.

services.name: Problem Management
services.description: Underlying cause analysis and known-error management (conventionally PRB-prefixed identifiers). Problems group recurring incidents, document root cause and workarounds, and link forward to changes. When summarizing or reasoning across modules, related problem and known-error context may appear alongside incident or change narratives. Select problem when the active entity or user intent targets problem management, not when the record is purely an incident or request.

services.name: Change Management
services.description: Controlled production and infrastructure changes (conventionally CHG-prefixed identifiers). Used for scheduled maintenance, releases, and risk-managed modifications with approval and window semantics that differ from incidents. Choose change when the ticket or conversation centers on a change record, CAB-driven work, or post-incident remediation delivered as a formal change—not an ad hoc incident ticket.

services.name: Asset Management
services.description: Hardware and software asset records (laptops, monitors, licenses, etc.) that can be linked from incidents, requests, or other work. This service_id scopes read-only or policy-governed asset context—such as summarizing an asset mentioned on a ticket—without treating the conversation as an asset-ticket type. Use asset when the primary payload is asset metadata or when summarization explicitly includes related asset rows.

services.name: Configuration Management Database
services.description: Configuration items and their relationships within the CMDB: servers, applications, network elements, and dependencies. Provides structured CI context for impact analysis, summarization of linked infrastructure, and knowledge tied to a configuration item. Use cmdb when the user or ticket context targets CI-centric views or when tools are parameterized for CMDB reads rather than a generic incident or request module.

services.name: Knowledge Management
services.description: Published and governed knowledge content: how-to articles, known errors surfaced as KB, and searchable documentation. Drives NL query, ticket-context, and article-by-id retrieval paths. This service_id anchors agents and tools that search or fetch KB records; it does not represent an actionable incident or request record by itself unless the user explicitly opens an article or asks a knowledge-only question.

services.name: Service Catalog
services.description: The service catalog: orderable offerings, bundled tasks, approval chains, and templates that expand into fulfillment work. Distinct from ad hoc incidents and from generic “request” tickets that already exist; catalog is the module for browsing, configuring, and submitting standardized services. Fulfillment and creation flows that start from a catalog item or template should resolve service_id to catalog where the registry row and agent mapping expect it.

services.name: Employee Onboarding
services.description: Joiner and lifecycle onboarding programs that decompose into ordered tasks (HR, IT, access, equipment), often spanning catalog items and manual checkpoints. Use onboarding when the active workflow or user intent is an onboarding journey rather than a single incident fix or a one-off catalog order; fulfillment agents map this service_id to long-running, task-graph execution patterns.
